Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Class LegacyBitmapRenderer

Namespace
Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services
Assembly
Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll

Basic monochrome bitmap renderer for Apple IIe video modes (stopgap implementation).

public class LegacyBitmapRenderer : IDisplayBitmapRenderer
Inheritance
object
LegacyBitmapRenderer
Implements
IDisplayBitmapRenderer
Inherited Members
object.Equals(object)
object.Equals(object, object)
object.GetHashCode()
object.GetType()
object.MemberwiseClone()
object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.ToString()

Remarks

⚠️ TEMPORARY IMPLEMENTATION: This renderer is a stopgap solution providing basic Apple IIe monochrome bitmap output. It will be replaced by a more accurate scanline-based renderer in a future update.

Monochrome Output: This renderer (and its replacement) generates MONOCHROME BITMAPS ONLY. NTSC color artifact generation is handled by downstream UI renderers, not by this class. The output is a binary (on/off) bitmap representing what pixels would be lit on a monochrome monitor.

Supported Modes:

  • Text Mode: 40-column (fully supported), 80-column (basic support)
  • Lo-Res Graphics: 40×48 blocks (fully supported)
  • Hi-Res Graphics: 280×192 pixels (basic support)
  • Mixed Mode: Graphics + 4-line text status area (supported)

Known Limitations:

  • Cell-based rendering instead of scanline-by-scanline
  • Simplified pixel fringing logic
  • Double hi-res (DHGR) partially implemented
  • No accurate timing simulation

Rendering Approach: This renderer uses a cell-based approach, iterating through 24 rows × 40 columns and rendering each 8-scanline cell based on the current video mode. Memory addresses are calculated using Apple IIe's interleaved scanline pattern, and pixels are written to bitplane 0 of the frame buffer.

Constructors

LegacyBitmapRenderer(ICharacterRomProvider)

Initializes a new instance of the LegacyBitmapRenderer class.

public LegacyBitmapRenderer(ICharacterRomProvider charRomProvider)

Parameters

charRomProvider ICharacterRomProvider

Character ROM provider for text mode rendering. Provides 8-row glyph data for each character, with support for flashing characters, MouseText, and alternate character set.

Exceptions

ArgumentNullException

Thrown if charRomProvider is null.

Methods

Render(RenderContext)

Renders the current Apple IIe video state into the provided frame buffer.

public void Render(RenderContext context)

Parameters

context RenderContext

A RenderContext containing the frame buffer to render into, direct memory access for reading video RAM, and system status information including video mode soft switches.

Remarks

This method examines the video mode flags in the context (text/graphics, mixed mode, hi-res/lo-res, page selection) and renders the appropriate video content:

  • Reads from the correct video memory page (page 1 or page 2) based on soft switches
  • Interprets memory contents according to the active video mode
  • Handles character ROM lookups for text mode, including flashing characters and MouseText
  • Applies Apple IIe color generation rules for graphics modes
  • Renders mixed mode by combining graphics and text regions
  • Outputs pixels to the frame buffer in a format ready for display

This method should be called once per video frame (typically 60 times per second) to keep the display synchronized with video memory changes. The rendering process directly reads memory through IDirectMemoryPoolReader to access both main and auxiliary video pages without going through the CPU bus.
